message:
So last week the power went out for like four hours, and I spent the first 20 minutes wandering around my house like a confused ghost, opening the fridge repeatedly (which I know makes it worse but HABIT), bumping into furniture, and finally locating one sad flashlight that had maybe 30% battery life left. My kid asked 'Mom, why do we never have flashlights when we need them?' and honestly... fair point.

I realized I'd never actually *thought* about what being without power actually looks like in my house. No phone chargers. No backup light. Food that needed refrigeration. It wasn't a disaster—we were fine—but it was that perfect little reality check that made me think 'okay, we could do better here.'

Turns out you don't need to go full doomsday mode. Just a few basics—a couple of decent flashlights or a lantern, maybe a portable charger for phones, some shelf-stable snacks—sitting in an actual accessible place (not buried in the garage), makes a genuinely huge difference in staying calm and comfortable if it happens again.

Have you ever had that 'oh wow, we're not ready' moment? What did you end up doing? Found a good refresher on this stuff that's worth bookmarking.
